channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Iranian Hackers Target Siemens and Schneider Industrial Systems, CISA Warns
item.description: US government agencies have warned that Iranian cyber actors are targeting US-based Siemens and Schneider industrial equipment

item.title: Nuclear-Sabotage Malware Benchmark Trips Up Most Frontier AI Models
item.description: <p>SentinelOne’s new benchmark, built on the Fast16 case, shows which AI models can sustain a malware investigation and which cannot.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/nuclear-sabotage-malware-benchmark-trips-up-most-frontier-ai-models/">Nuclear-Sabotage Malware Benchmark Trips Up Most Frontier AI Models</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
item.description: Most people understand identity theft as an attacker stealing a real person's sensitive information and impersonating them. Synthetic identity fraud is much harder to catch. Instead of stealing a real identity, the attacker manufactures a new one, frankensteining together several real data points with fabricated ones to create a person who doesn't exist. Since no real victim monitors misuse, a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Agents Now the Enterprises Fastest Growing Exposed Attack Surface
item.description: Sophos report warns that the rapid adoption of AI by businesses is leaving them vulnerable to a new source of cyber threats

item.title: Attackers Weaponize GitHub Actions Runners to Target cPanel and WHM Servers
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a large-scale campaign that has turned compromised GitHub repositories into distributed attack infrastructure designed to target cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) instances.
The activity involves malicious Packagist development versions spanning 10 packages associated with a legitimate PHP and DevOps developer, dinushchathurya, between July 12 and 13,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Agentic AI Challenges Progress in Confidential Computing
item.description: Core issues that slowed down adoption of secure data vaults are being resolved by technology, but artificial intelligence poses new ones. Experts have some answers.
item.dc:creator: Agam Shah

item.title: End-to-End Encryption and “Going Dark”
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses
item.description: <p>Hackers recently obtained non-sensitive customer information and other documents from the company.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/upbound-group-says-data-breach-led-to-13-million-in-fraudulent-contract-losses/">Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: New Dolphin X Stealer Employs AI Profiling to Prioritize Targets
item.description: Dolphin X is a new infostealer that uses AI to sort and rank victims, giving cybercriminals a faster way to identify lucrative targets

item.title: Google Adds Selfie Video Recovery for Users Locked Out of Their Accounts
item.description:
Google on Thursday announced a new way for users to sign-in to their accounts by letting them take a selfie video.
The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing recovery methods to log in to an account, including an email address or a phone number. The idea is to use a video selfie as a way to regain access if a user ever gets locked out or doesn't have access
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Assaf Keren Appointed New CISO of Meta
item.description: <p>He replaces Guy Rosen, who announced his retirement from the company after 13 years.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/assaf-keren-appointed-new-ciso-of-meta/">Assaf Keren Appointed New CISO of Meta</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: <p>The vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-16232 has been exploited against customers with certain configurations.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/new-check-point-zero-day-vulnerability-exploited-in-the-wild/">New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
item.description:
RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root access.
Qualys said default installations of Red Hat Enterprise Linux and its derivatives, Fedora Server, and Amazon Linux can meet the conditions for exploitation.
The company demonstrated the race
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Two-Thirds of Ransomware Victims Say AI Boosted Attack Effectiveness
item.description: A new study of organizations which have fallen victim to ransomware suggests the rise of AI-tools being used by hackers is making life harder for defenders

item.title: Brazilian Banking Trojan Actively Spreading in Portugal
item.description: Portuguese businesses operate in the same native language as Brazilian hackers, making those businesses easy targets.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Check Point Patches Exploited SmartConsole Flaw Allowing Full Admin Access
item.description:
Check Point has released security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities impacting Security Management and Multi-Domain Management (MDSM) products, including a critical flaw that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The security flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-16232 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an authentication bypass affecting the Check Point SmartConsole login process that allows an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: US Warns of Iranian Hackers Targeting Siemens, Schneider, and Rockwell ICS Devices
item.description: <p>An updated advisory from federal agencies provides information on the techniques used to hack programmable logic controllers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/us-warns-of-iranian-hackers-targeting-siemens-schneider-and-rockwell-ics-devices/">US Warns of Iranian Hackers Targeting Siemens, Schneider, and Rockwell ICS Devices</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Ransomware Attack Puts a Chill On Japanese Frozen-Food Chain
item.description: A cyberattack on a food and logistics firm disrupts the supply of frozen food to thousands of clients, including major franchises like Kentucky Fried Chicken.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Ransomware Incident Response Checklist | UpGuard
item.description: Navigate modern ransomware and double extortion with this 5-phase incident response checklist based on NIST guidelines. Contain, recover, and harden today.

item.title: Oracle Just Shipped 1,449 Security Patches in One Quarter. We Checked How Much of It Is Actually New. | UpGuard
item.description: Oracle's July 2026 update shipped 1,449 security patches — 2.8x its all-time record. We parsed 23 quarters of advisories to find out how much is truly new.

item.title: Flaws in Passkey Implementation Show Old Attacks Still Work
item.description: Ahead of Black Hat USA, researchers find exploitable flaws in how Microsoft handles passkeys that could allow attackers to impersonate privileged users.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain
item.description: Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Fake Bahrain Alert App Deploys Android Surveillance Malware
item.description: A malicious application delivers four-stage Android spyware via phony Google Play sites, exploiting civilian fear during Iranian missile strikes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Third-Party SDKs Raise Privacy Questions for Apps Marketed to U.S. Military
item.description: <p>Researchers found Chinese and Russian SDKs in Android apps marketed to U.S. military users, highlighting software supply chain and enterprise privacy risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-android-sdk-supply-chain-privacy-military-apps/">Third-Party SDKs Raise Privacy Questions for Apps Marketed to U.S. Military</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
item.description:
Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more.
Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's growing triage queue, will retain the previous payout terms.
GitHub said the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment.
The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8), impacts default installations of Ubuntu Desktop 24.04, 25.10, and 26.04. The disclosure comes as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: When AI Attacks: OpenAI Models Autonomously Hack Hugging Face
item.description: Advanced LLMs escaped their sandboxes while attempting to achieve a non-malicious benchmark test objective.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: New Data Shows Suno Breach Affected 55M Accounts
item.description: <p>New data shows 55.3 million Suno accounts were affected in a breach exposing contact details, purchases, and partial payment card information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-suno-breach-affected-55-million-users/">New Data Shows Suno Breach Affected 55M Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts
item.description: <p>Hackers leaked names, email addresses, phone numbers, passwords, and financial information stolen from the two platforms. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/suno-paidwork-data-breaches-affect-tens-of-millions-of-accounts/">Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a silent hijack of a user's WhatsApp data.
The shortcoming has been codenamed HermeticReader by Guardio Labs. It's officially tracked as CVE-2026-48294 (CVSS score: 7.4), with the vulnerability
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: TrickBot Ditches HTTP for DNS Tunneling in Latest Variant
item.description: New TrickBot variant hides C2 communication inside DNS queries, replacing decade-old HTTP pattern

item.title: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Observability Platform Provider Embrace
item.description: <p>Acquisition follows January's Chronosphere deal, deepening Palo Alto Networks' push beyond core security into observability.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/palo-alto-networks-to-acquire-observability-platform-provider-embrace/">Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Observability Platform Provider Embrace</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Flaw in Adobe Extension With 300M Installs Enabled WhatsApp Data Theft
item.description: <p>An attacker only needed to convince the targeted user to visit a malicious website to exfiltrate WhatsApp messages and contacts.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/flaw-in-adobe-extension-with-300m-installs-enabled-whatsapp-data-theft/">Flaw in Adobe Extension With 300M Installs Enabled WhatsApp Data Theft</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: When Identity Verification Fails: Lessons from a Real-World SIM Swap and Near Account Takeover
item.description: <p>Identity confidence changes throughout every interaction and should be reassessed continuously as new risk signals emerge.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/when-identity-verification-fails-lessons-from-a-real-world-sim-swap-and-near-account-takeover/">When Identity Verification Fails: Lessons from a Real-World SIM Swap and Near Account Takeover</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Torsten George

item.title: StrongestLayer Raises $4.1 Million in Seed Funding Extension
item.description: <p>The startup will use the fresh investment to accelerate its go-to-market strategy and to expand its platform.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/strongestlayer-raises-4-1-million-in-seed-funding-extension/">StrongestLayer Raises $4.1 Million in Seed Funding Extension</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
item.description:
A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case of unauthenticated path traversal impacting Windmill's "get_log_file" endpoint ("/api/w/{workspace}/jobs_u/get_log_file/{filename}").
"The filename parameter is concatenated into
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security
item.description:
Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need, employees the tools they want, and CISOs the strategic influence they have earned.
According to McKinsey's State of AI report, 76 percent of employees now use AI in some capacity at work, up from 55
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Open AI Claims Its AI Models Went Rogue and Hacked Another Company
item.description: Hugging Face recently disclosed a security breach. OpenAI has now said that it was its AI models which broke containment and hacked Hugging Face themselves

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description:
OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugging Face's production infrastructure last week.
The AI company said the models were operating with "reduced cyber refusals for evaluation purposes" that might otherwise limit their ability to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: EU Financial Institutions Leak Data Through Cookie Trackers
item.description: European and US banks inadvertently transmitted customer data to ad platforms via tracking pixels, raising serious compliance, security, and privacy concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Why Modern SOCs Need Multi-Layered Detections
item.description:
The cycle is over. For years, cybersecurity followed a familiar pattern: defenses improved, attackers adapted, and the back-and-forth continued. Today, AI-equipped attackers are simply outpacing defenses. Most intrusions now bypass endpoint and malware-based detection entirely.
The CrowdStrike Global Threat Report estimates around 79% of attacks are malware-free, as threat actors rely on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: First-Person Identity Theft Story
item.description: <p>Harrowing <a href="https://tech.yahoo.com/cybersecurity/articles/stranger-used-one-text-message-140003797.html">story</a> of an identity theft victim.</p> <p>Yes, the person made a mistake&#8212;they gave the scammer a two-factor authentication code that allowed the scammer to take over their email address. But the real story here is how, for many of us, the security of most of our accounts hangs on the security of our email accounts.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Vulnerability Enables Local Root Access
item.description: New Ubuntu snap-confine race condition lets local users escalate to root on default installs

item.title: Google Makes CodeMender Available as Managed AI Security Agent
item.description: CodeMender actively builds and runs exploits in customer-managed sandboxes to verify if vulnerabilities are truly exploitable

item.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
item.description:
German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authorities arrested the man they say developed and ran it.
In a joint announcement on Monday, the Frankfurt public prosecutor's cybercrime unit (ZIT) and Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (BKA)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Proofpoint Research Finds 65% of Organizations Affected by Ransomware Say AI Made Attacks More Effective
Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live game results on Digitain.
The package, named "Newtonsoftt.Json.Net," masquerades as the Newtonsoft.Json library and is a trojanized fork. Seven versions of the package have been published to the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description:
A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what it finds.
The flaw is in Microsoft's official Azure DevOps MCP server, and it works because one of its tools returns pull request descriptions without a prompt-injection guardrail the company had
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less than a month after researchers found that more than 42 percent of games and other apps available for download on LG's webOS store allow unknown third-parties to route their Internet traffic through a user's TV.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, but It's Not Because of AI
item.description: Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Using LLMs to Find and Prioritize Vulnerabilities Is No Easy Task
item.description: The latest large language models have high false-positive rates and fail to take into account the context of scans, leading to more work for AppSec professionals.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
item.description:
Apple has moved to address a security flaw in its Hide My Email service that enabled users' real email addresses to be unmasked, effectively undermining the feature's privacy guarantees.
404 Media reported Tuesday that a fix for the issue was deployed by Apple on July 3, 2026, after more than a year, when it was disclosed to the company by Tyler Murphy, co-founder of EasyOptOuts.
Hide My Email
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacker Turns AI Jailbreaks Into Offensive Attack Platform
item.description: A Russian-speaking actor, &quot;Trim,&quot; dismantled publicly available frontier models and integrated them with offensive security tools.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Hackers Already Exploiting Newly Patched WordPress Flaws, Researchers Warn
item.description: <p>Security researchers warn hackers are actively exploiting two patched WordPress Core vulnerabilities that could let attackers fully compromise unpatched websites.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-wordpress-core-vulnerabilities-active-exploitation/">Hackers Already Exploiting Newly Patched WordPress Flaws, Researchers Warn</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
item.description:
Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to stop it.
Intezer, in research with Kodem Security, found that a request as ordinary as asking Kiro to summarize a page could end in remote code execution. AWS has patched the issue, and no CVE has been
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently.
According to the tech giant, the model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender as part of a limited-access pilot
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-50522 Under Active Exploitation After Public PoC
item.description:
A third SharePoint Server flaw patched by Microsoft as part of its Patch Tuesday update for July 2026 has come under active exploitation, per watchTowr.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-50522 (CVSS score: 9.8), a critical deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Office SharePoint that could allow an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network. Microsoft credited DEVCORE
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Craneware Confirms Data Theft After Cyberattack, Investigations Underway
item.description: <p>Healthcare software vendor Craneware confirmed attackers stole data during a cyberattack, underscoring growing cybersecurity risks facing healthcare suppliers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-craneware-cyberattack-data-theft-2026/">Craneware Confirms Data Theft After Cyberattack, Investigations Underway</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments.
Arctic Wolf Labs said it investigated multiple intrusions in June 2026 that began with the exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw affecting the portal and gateway
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Russian Hacker Turns Jailbroken Claude Into Pentest Platform
item.description: Russian-speaking actor Trim built a commercial offensive AI pentest tool on jailbroken Claude models

item.title: Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Zimbra has rolled out fixes to address multiple critical security issues, including a command injection flaw in the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) monitoring component.
As many as nine security vulnerabilities have been patched in Zimbra 10.1.20. Topping the list is a command injection vulnerability in the SNMP monitoring component when SNMP notifications are enabled.
Also patched
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Choose Wisely: AI-Generated Coding Risk Varies, a Lot
item.description: AI-generated code introduces 15 vulnerabilities on average per codebase, but the actual risk depends on framework pairing more than the model used.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: A New Ransomware Threat Actor Emerges Every Week, Warns Report
item.description: Analysis by Black Kite warns that ransomware ecosystem is becoming bigger and more fragmented

item.title: FBI Warns of Deepfake Videos Impersonating IC3 Leadership
item.description: FBI warned of deepfake videos of IC3 leadership directing users to spoofed complaint sites

item.title: Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
item.description:
An Android app that can draw over other windows and write to shared storage can slip instructions to the AI agent driving that phone, in text no human eye will ever see. Two more steps, and the same app is running commands on the PC driving the agent.
Researchers demonstrated that chain, plus six other attacks, against five open-source mobile agent frameworks: AppAgent, AppAgentX,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.
item.description:
Every patch is a confession.
The moment a vendor ships a security fix, the diff between the old code and the new code tells anyone watching exactly what was broken and where. Turn that diff back into a working exploit, and you can hit every system that hasn't updated yet. This is N-day exploitation, and it's always been a race: the vendor patches, the clock starts, and defenders try to deploy
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
item.description:
A cloud tenant using nothing but ordinary GPU access can push a data center's power draw up and down fast enough to threaten the grid it runs on, with no exploit and no break-in.
That is the claim behind Bit2Watt, described by three Zhejiang University researchers in a paper accepted to CHES 2026, the IACR's hardware-security conference, and the evidence splits in two: they measured the power
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: MIT to Become Hotbed of AI Video Surveillance
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: US Hospital Finance Software Provider Craneware Reports Data Theft
item.description: Craneware, a provider of financial software for US healthcare organizations, has disclosed a cyber incident involving unauthorized access and data theft

item.title: Researchers Uncover North Korean 'ClickFake' Campaign Targeting Web3 Pros
item.description: In a new campaign, North Korean hacking group Famous Chollima targeted crypto professionals through ClickFix lures to deliver Windows and macOS trojans

item.title: WordPress wp2shell Exploitation Grows as Public Exploit Fuels Mass Scanning
item.description:
Attackers have begun to exploit two critical vulnerabilities in WordPress that, when combined together, enable unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) and complete compromise of vulnerable websites.
The two security flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137, have been codenamed wp2shell.
"By the early hours of Saturday morning (UTC), successful exploitation was already well
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New ENCFORGE Ransomware Targets AI Model Files in Langflow RCE Attack
item.description:
Researchers at Sysdig have linked a second attack on the same Langflow server to JADEPUFFER, the AI-agent-driven operator it first documented earlier this month.
The same operator has now been spotted deploying ENCFORGE, a new compiled Go ransomware designed to encrypt model weights, vector indexes, training datasets, and other AI infrastructure files across the host filesystem.
The entry
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Best Tools for Securing MCP and LLM Integrations | UpGuard
item.description: Secure your AI infrastructure. Learn how to protect shadow MCP and LLM integrations using the best tools for attack surface discovery and runtime defenses.

item.title: Best Threat Intelligence Platforms and Vendors | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the best threat intelligence platforms (TIPs) and vendors of 2026. Learn key features, criteria, and find the perfect tool for your security team.

item.title: Data leakage risks with DBHub MCP servers | UpGuard
item.description: UpGuard found exposed DBHub servers leaking live databases to the open internet—an early sign that MCP exposure is becoming a systemic data risk.

item.title: Best Cyber Threat Detection Tools & Software | UpGuard
item.description: The best cyber threat detection tools and software in 2026. Learn how to protect your endpoints, logs, and external digital surfaces from a breach.

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools & Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Discover the best vulnerability management tools on the market and learn which vulnerability detection solution is best for your organization.

item.title: Best Data Breach & Data Leak Detection Tools | UpGuard
item.description: Stopping an intrusion is half the battle. This guide ranks the top breach and leak detection tools to neutralize exposed credentials before attackers do.

item.title: Critical ServiceNow AI Platform Flaw Exploited for Unauthenticated Code Execution
item.description:
Threat actors are now exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting ServiceNow AI Platform, according to Defused Cyber.
In a post shared on X, the threat intelligence firm said it's observing in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-6875 (CVSS score: 9.5), a sandbox escape vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated user to run arbitrary code.
Patches for the flaw were
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 'WP2Shell' Opens Millions of WordPress Sites to Remote Takeover
item.description: Barely three days after disclosure, attackers are widely chaining together CVE-2026-60137 and CVE-2026-63030 to lob exploit attempts against one of the largest attack surfaces on the Internet.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Remediating Vulnerabilities With LLMs: Inside Ivanti's Automation Push
item.description: Ivanti CSO Daniel Spicer says frontier models have shown surprising effectiveness in early stages, but cost and human-in-the-loop viability remain open questions.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: 25 Years After Code Red: What the Worm Era Can Teach Us About AI Security
item.description: Marc Maiffret reflects on Code Red's legacy and the security lessons helping organizations navigate AI risk today.
item.dc:creator: Marc Maiffret, James Maude, Dennis Fisher

item.title: CISOs Feel the Heat Over AI Risk
item.description: Job pressures have increased as companies run headlong into AI adoption, causing 26% of top security executives to consider leaving their position.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Attackers Combo Up Evasion Tactics for BEC Phishing
item.description: &quot;The TFF Trap&quot; uses fileless techniques and loaders with low detection rates to deploy various RATs and stealers, including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: FakeGit Campaign Uses 7,600 GitHub Repositories to Spread SmartLoader Malware
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered nearly 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories, out of which more than 800 pose as artificial intelligence (AI) skills or Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to deliver a malware family known as SmartLoader as part of an ongoing campaign codenamed FakeGit.
"FakeGit uses copied projects, lookalike developer profiles, convincing READMEs, and malicious ZIP
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
item.description:
A malware operator left its delivery server wide open, and Rapid7 pulled down the whole toolkit: 1,048 files spanning lure templates, filename-spoofing tests, execution experiments, droppers, builder notes, and two campaign chains. One was already live against Windows users in Mexico, delivering an infostealer through a fake government ID-lookup site over WebDAV.
What makes it more than a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AWS Billion-Dollar Software Bug Explained
item.description: <p>An AWS software bug showed some customers billing estimates in the billions and trillions. Here is what failed, why invoices were unaffected, and what IT teams should know.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-aws-billing-bug-trillion-dollar-estimates-explained/">AWS Billion-Dollar Software Bug Explained</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Best Cyber Risk Posture Management (CRPM) Platforms | UpGuard
item.description: Learn what Cyber Risk Posture Management (CRPM) is, what it isn't, and explore the top CRPM platforms built for lean security teams in 2026.

item.title: 2026 North America Community Meeting Agenda Highlights
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Cruciferra Crypter Uses Process Ghosting to Evade Detection
item.description: Cruciferra crypter used process ghosting and 90 custom ciphers to hide payloads for multiple actors

item.title: HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050
item.description:
A newly discovered espionage implant has been using a hijacked Microsoft 365 calendar as its command channel, planting operator instructions and smuggling out stolen files as attachments on calendar events dated to the year 2050.
Group-IB, which named the malware HollowGraph, says the approach moves tasking and stolen data through legitimate Microsoft Graph API traffic, so the activity looks
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: JadePuffer Returns With Ransomware Designed to Wipe AI Models
item.description: JadePuffer follow-up campaign deployed ENCFORGE locker built to destroy AI model artifacts

item.title: Cybersecurity Keeps Events 'Uneventful'
item.description: From the World Cup to the United States' 250th celebration, this year's event calendar has been packed with high-profile gatherings that drew global audiences, intense scrutiny, and enormous security demands.
item.dc:creator: Olga Polishchuk

item.title: Researchers Build WordPress Exploit Using OpenAI's GPT
item.description: A researcher who discovered a critical vulnerability in WordPress has used OpenAI’s latest model to develop an exploit chain

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: WordPress RCE, SonicWall 0-Days, AI Service Attacks, SharePoint 0-Day and More
item.description:
A single request should not be able to do this much. But this week, small inputs led to code execution, memory loss, stolen keys, and disabled security tools.
The paths were often simple: exposed systems, weak checks, old drivers, fake prompts, and public code used for malware delivery. Some bugs were new. Others were already being used before defenders had time to patch.
Here is the full
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New HollowGraph Malware Hijacks Microsoft 365 Calendars for Covert C2 Communications
item.description: Researchers have linked HollowGraph malware to the Cavern framework after discovering its use of Microsoft 365 calendars and Microsoft Graph APIs as a stealthy C2 channel

item.title: Russian Intelligence Hacks IP Cameras to Spy on Military Logistics Across NATO States and Ukraine
item.description:
At least one Russian intelligence service is systematically hijacking internet-connected security cameras across Europe and Ukraine, using the feeds to watch military transport routes, weapons shipments bound for Kyiv, and the locations of Ukrainian troops.
That is the finding of a cybersecurity advisory published July 10 by the AIVD and MIVD, the Netherlands' civilian and military intelligence
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mythos Didn't Break Your Security Program. Your Exposure Window Could.
item.description: The industry spent the initial months after Anthropic's April 7 Mythos reveal focused on volume. How many new CVEs would Mythos add to an already overloaded pipeline? How quickly would the flood of AI-driven discovery overwhelm triage capabilities? How long would it take adversaries to weaponize Mythos findings at scale? Those questions were and remain valid. Yet they all stop short of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: On Flock License Plate Tracking Cameras
item.description: <p>A recent story of a writer who was <a href="https://www.thedrive.com/news/how-flock-cameras-wrongly-tracked-me-for-days-over-stolen-plates-and-sent-police-after-me">mistakenly</a> identified, tracked, and arrested using data from Flock cameras has gone viral.</p> <blockquote><p>The New Jersey plates that were allegedly stolen from the LA dealer were <b>34 03 DTM</b>, not <b>34 10 DTM</b>. But when the police report was created and the plate was entered into Flock’s system, it was just recorded as <b>34 DTM</b>. Just the five large characters, no little number in the middle. And Flock’s AI tech wasn’t registering that non-standard little number when it began picking up the Range Rover around town. It just saw ...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Police Chiefs Cite TfL Hack in Push for Cybercrime Risk Orders
item.description: Two chiefs of UK policing agencies said the Transport for London prosecution demonstrates the need for Cybercrime Risk Orders
